artifacts.summary: Official University of Bayreuth discovery found a central ChatGPT status page and an ITS AI-use page with substantive access and data-handling guidance. Faculty and ZHL pages show limited local discussion or training, while no central binding AI assessment policy was identified in official-domain search.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Bayreuth has central official pages discussing ChatGPT and ITS-supported AI access, but this crawl did not identify a central binding university-wide AI assessment policy.

artifacts.claimText: University of Bayreuth ITS says members can use existing licenses to access ChatGPT and DALL-E through Microsoft Copilot with their University of Bayreuth bt account, and can also use GenAI open-source models through AcademicCloud.

artifacts.claimText: University of Bayreuth ITS advises that official Copilot login provides commercial data protection and no model-training assurance, but Copilot for Web/Edge is not recommended for personal or confidential data and prompts should not contain personal data or disclose confidential content.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a cautious source-status claim; it explicitly avoids asserting a binding university-wide AI assessment policy.
Approved with narrow wording tied to official ITS and central university pages.
Approved because the claim tracks explicit ITS language about official login, commercial data protection, and personal/confidential data limits.
Approved as training/support availability, not as proof of completed compliance or binding academic policy.

artifacts.title: University of Bayreuth AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official sources show University of Bayreuth central and IT guidance on ChatGPT, Copilot, DALL-E, AcademicCloud GenAI access, AI literacy training, and data-handling limits. The staged review did not identify a central binding AI assessment policy; faculty and ZHL pages were treated as limited context only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Faculty, department, seminar, and event pages were not used to infer university-wide binding policy.
The law-faculty PDF was not used as evidence because extraction did not yield substantive source-language text.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
